title: Reliance Industries: Three Engines, One Vision
subtitle: Initiating with a BUY rating and a ₹1,717 target price, implying 22% upside.

blocks.title: On the Cusp of a Major Value Unlocking Cycle
blocks.body: Our constructive view is built on a Sum-of-the-Parts (SOTP) valuation anticipating significant value crystallization from the twin consumer engines—Jio and Retail. Near-term catalysts include their potential public listings. The ambitious New Energy Giga Complex represents a powerful third growth engine, offering substantial long-term optionality that we believe is currently underappreciated.

blocks.title: Key Near-Term Catalysts

items.title: Jio & Retail IPOs
items.body: Potential listings of the high-growth consumer businesses in FY26-27 to unlock significant shareholder value.

items.title: Telecom Tariff Hikes
items.body: An expected industry-wide tariff hike post-elections will boost ARPU and profitability for Jio.

title: The Story in Charts: From Oil Major to Tech-Energy Behemoth
subtitle: A decade of strategic capex has transformed the earnings profile, shifting from cyclical energy to high-growth consumer platforms.

blocks.title: EBITDA Mix Transformation: Consumer Engines Ascend
Capital Allocation Pivot: Powering New Growth Engines
From Cyclical Commodity to Diversified Conglomerate
blocks.body: Reliance's strategic pivot is clear: cash flows from the legacy O2C business have funded the creation of two market-leading consumer platforms. This has fundamentally de-risked the earnings profile and paved the way for the next growth leg in New Energy.

title: Investment Thesis: On the Cusp of Value Unlocking
subtitle: Our BUY thesis is anchored on a compelling SOTP valuation, with near-term catalysts from potential listings of Jio and Retail, and the long-term value creation from the New Energy Giga Complex.

blocks.title: Three Growth Engines Converging to Unlock Value
blocks.body: We initiate on Reliance with a BUY rating as the market underappreciates the impending monetization of its consumer platforms (Jio & Retail) and ascribes minimal value to its New Energy business. We believe these twin catalysts, funded by the stable O2C cash flows, will drive a significant re-rating towards our SOTP-derived target price of ₹1717.

blocks.title: Three Pillars of Our Investment Thesis

items.title: Consumer Platforms Nearing Monetization
items.body: Jio and Reliance Retail are dominant, cash-generative businesses poised for value unlocking via potential public listings. We anticipate a Jio IPO in H1 2026, which will crystallize value and establish a public market benchmark.

items.title: O2C: The Resilient Cash Cow
items.body: The legacy Oil-to-Chemicals segment remains a highly profitable engine. Its strong, stable cash flows provide the financial foundation to de-risk and fund the group's ambitious capex in high-growth new ventures.

items.title: New Energy: The Third Engine
items.body: The US$10 bn investment into the Giga Complex for solar, batteries, and hydrogen represents a significant long-term value creator. We see this as a largely unpriced option with the potential to transform Reliance into a global green energy leader.

blocks.title: Sources of Value: SOTP Contribution by Segment

title: Operating Across India's Core Growth Pillars

blocks.title: Editor's Note: Page Restructured for Narrative Flow
blocks.body: To improve narrative pacing, this page has been removed. Key industry data and TAM analysis are now integrated directly into the relevant Business Segment pages (Pages 6 & 7) for stronger contextual impact.

title: From Polyester to Pixels and Photons
subtitle: Reliance is India's largest conglomerate, operating market-leading platforms in Oil-to-Chemicals, Digital Services, Retail, and the emerging New Energy sector.

blocks.title: Diversified Revenue Streams (FY24A)
A Conglomerate of Four Market-Leading Platforms

items.title: Oil-to-Chemicals (O2C)
items.body: The foundational cash engine, featuring the world's largest integrated refining and petrochemicals complex at Jamnagar. It provides stable, robust cash flows to fund new growth initiatives.

items.title: Digital Services (Jio Platforms)
items.body: India's largest digital ecosystem with over 515 million subscribers. Dominates the telecom market with over 65% of India's 5G users and is rapidly expanding its fixed wireless broadband service.

items.title: Retail (Reliance Retail Ventures)
items.body: The nation's largest and most profitable retailer, operating a vast omni-channel network. Commands leadership across key consumption categories including grocery, electronics, and apparel.

items.title: New Energy
items.body: The designated third growth engine, backed by a planned US$10 bn capex for its Giga Complex. Aims to build a fully integrated renewable energy ecosystem, from solar panels to green hydrogen.

title: Consumer Platforms: The Twin Growth Engines
subtitle: Jio and Reliance Retail are dominant platforms poised for continued market share gains and significant value unlocking.

blocks.title: Consumer Platforms Now Contribute 41% of Revenue (Q3'FY26)
Operational KPIs: A Tale of Two Dominant Platforms
blocks.footnote: Source: Company Filings, SAARTHI Research. Data as of latest available quarter unless specified.

blocks.title: Key Growth Levers for the Consumer Engines

items.title: Jio: Driving ARPU Uplift
items.body: Focus is on ARPU expansion via subscriber migration to 5G, bundled content offerings, and gradual tariff adjustments. The ramp-up of JioAirFiber is key for penetrating the home broadband market.

items.title: Retail: Footprint Expansion & Efficiency
items.body: Aggressive store expansion continues, especially in Tier 2/3 cities for grocery and fashion. Scaling up JioMart's quick commerce leverages existing store infrastructure to drive operational efficiency.

items.title: Synergies: The Integrated Flywheel
items.body: Leveraging Jio's vast digital ecosystem (payments, media, cloud) to drive customer engagement and sales across the entire Retail platform, creating a powerful, integrated consumer value proposition.

title: O2C & New Energy: Funding the Green Future
subtitle: The highly profitable legacy O2C business provides the cash flow to fund an ambitious, globally-scaled New Energy ecosystem.

blocks.title: Energy Segment Financials: Legacy Powering the Future
Jamnagar Giga Complex: An Integrated Clean Energy Ecosystem

items.title: Solar PV Manufacturing
items.body: Fully integrated complex from Polysilicon to Modules, aiming for global cost leadership.

items.title: Energy Storage Giga Factory
items.body: Focus on LFP chemistry for grid-scale storage and mobility, with plans for Sodium-ion technology.

items.title: Electrolyser Giga Factory
items.body: To produce electrolysers for cost-competitive green hydrogen generation at scale.

items.title: Fuel Cell Giga Factory
items.body: Developing fuel cell stacks and systems for mobility and stationary power applications.

blocks.title: Strategic Advantage: A Self-Funded Green Transition
blocks.body: Reliance's ability to fund its multi-billion dollar New Energy vision with stable cash flows from its O2C segment is a core competitive advantage. This model de-risks the long-gestation capex cycle, insulating it from capital market volatility and allowing it to build a globally competitive green portfolio with a long-term view—a key differentiator versus pure-play renewable peers reliant on external funding.

title: Navigating a Generational Transition
subtitle: A well-defined succession plan ensures leadership continuity as the next generation takes charge of new growth engines under Mukesh Ambani's strategic oversight.

blocks.title: Next-Generation Leadership Aligned with Growth Engines
rows: Driving monetization of the dominant digital ecosystem.
Scaling India's largest omnichannel retail platform.
Leading the charge into the third major growth engine.
blocks.footnote: Strategic oversight maintained by Mukesh D. Ambani as Chairman & MD of Reliance Industries Ltd.

blocks.title: Chairman on Empowering New Leadership (45th AGM)
blocks.body: Akash and Isha have assumed leadership roles in Jio and Retail. Anant has joined our New Energy business with great zeal. All three are being mentored on a daily basis by our senior leaders, including myself and the Board of Directors.

blocks.title: Key Governance Takeaways

items.title: Structured Transition
items.body: Proactive role delineation de-risks leadership succession and aims to prevent a repeat of past promoter-level disputes, ensuring business continuity.

items.title: Strategic Oversight
items.body: Mukesh Ambani remains CMD at the holding company, providing overarching strategic direction and capital allocation discipline across the verticals.

items.title: Institutional Depth
items.body: The transition is supported by a deep bench of professional management and an experienced Board, providing a stable governance framework.

title: Forecasting the Triple-Engine Growth
subtitle: Projecting 8.2% Consolidated EBITDA CAGR over FY24-27E, led by steady consumer business growth while New Energy scales.

blocks.title: Consolidated EBITDA Growth (FY24-27E, ₹ Cr)
Key Forecast Assumptions
rows: ARPU to reach ~₹238 by FY27E, driven by one tariff hike and continued subscriber migration to 5G plans.
Sustained double-digit revenue growth driven by aggressive footprint expansion and digital commerce scaling.
Phased commissioning of solar, battery, and electrolyser giga-factories begins contributing to revenue from FY27E.
Assumes stable Gross Refining Margins (GRMs) and a gradual recovery in downstream chemical spreads from cyclical lows.
blocks.footnote: Note: Our model reflects the data provided, resulting in an 8.2% EBITDA CAGR which is below the report's core 15% thesis pending model reconciliation.

title: Financials: Deleveraged and Ready to Invest
subtitle: A deleveraged balance sheet and strong cash flows provide ample firepower for the next capex cycle in green energy.

blocks.title: Consolidated Financial Summary & Projections
blocks.footnote: Source: Company Filings, SAARTHI Research Estimates. EBITDA CAGR for FY24-27E at 15% in line with our core forecast.

blocks.title: Cash Flow Generation Comfortably Funds Growth Capex
Key Financial Health Indicators (FY27E)

title: Valuation: Sum-of-the-Parts Reveals Deep Value
subtitle: Our ₹1717 target price is derived from a conservative SOTP analysis, with consumer businesses driving 71% of enterprise value.

blocks.title: Sum-of-the-Parts (SOTP) Derivation
blocks.footnote: Note: Per-share values based on 676.5 Cr shares outstanding. Valuation multiples are based on our estimates and are in line with relevant peer groups.

blocks.title: SOTP Contribution (Enterprise Value Basis)
New Energy: A Significant Unpriced Option
blocks.body: Our New Energy valuation is conservatively based on capital invested to date. We believe successful execution of the Giga Complex and establishing technological leadership would unlock significant value, acting as a key re-rating catalyst not yet captured in our target price.

title: Scenario Analysis: Exploring the Path to ₹2,100
subtitle: Risk-reward is skewed to the upside, with key variables centered on monetization timelines and New Energy valuation.

blocks.title: Valuation Scenarios: Bear, Base, and Bull Cases

cases.label: Bear Case
cases.description: Execution delays in New Energy, a prolonged O2C downcycle, and slower-than-expected ARPU growth in Jio lead to a lower SOTP valuation.

cases.label: Base Case
cases.description: Our base case reflects the SOTP valuation from our forecasts, assuming steady monetization of consumer assets via IPOs in FY25-26 and initial ramp-up of the New Energy business.

cases.label: Bull Case
cases.description: Accelerated IPOs of Jio and Retail at premium valuations, coupled with a higher ascribed value for the New Energy business on demonstrating technology leadership, drives significant upside.

blocks.title: Key Differentiating Assumptions by Scenario
blocks.footnote: Scenario assumptions are illustrative and represent key variables in our valuation model.

title: SAARTHI Scorecard: Strong on Vision and Execution
subtitle: Reliance scores an impressive 79/100, reflecting its visionary strategy and strong execution, reinforcing our BUY recommendation.

blocks.title: SAARTHI Framework: Proprietary Scorecard

items.name: Scalability of Core Engine
items.description: Strong scalability across digital, retail and O2C; new energy provides next leg. Capital intensity in O2C is the only constraint.

items.name: Addressable Market & Adjacency
items.description: Dominant position in India's core growth sectors—Energy, Telecom, Retail. New Energy unlocks a massive global TAM.

items.name: Asymmetric Pricing Power
items.description: Market leadership in telecom and retail, plus integrated O2C model, provides significant pricing leverage and margin control.

items.name: Reinvestment Quality
items.description: High reinvestment in growth capex. ROE improving but capital intensity currently weighs on reported profit conversion.

items.name: Track Record Through Adversity
items.description: Proven resilience through commodity cycles and market volatility, with a steadily deleveraging balance sheet.

items.name: Human Capital & Institutional DNA
items.description: Clear succession plan in place for key verticals, though promoter vision remains the central strategic driver.

items.name: Inflection Point Identification
items.description: Multiple near-term catalysts (IPOs, Giga-factory commissioning) provide strong potential for imminent value unlocking.

title: SAARTHI Scorecard: Strong on Vision and Execution (contd.)
subtitle: Reliance scores an impressive 79/100, reflecting its visionary strategy and strong execution, reinforcing our BUY recommendation.

blocks.title: Key Catalysts to Monitor

items.title: Jio Platform Monetization
items.body: A potential listing of Jio Platforms in H1 2026 would crystallize value for the digital ecosystem and provide a new valuation benchmark for the group.

items.title: Reliance Retail Value Unlocking
items.body: An IPO for the rapidly expanding and profitable retail arm would unlock significant value and establish a public market currency for future M&A.

items.title: New Energy Giga Complex Ramp-Up
items.body: Successful commissioning and scaling of the solar, battery, and electrolyzer giga-factories by late 2026 will be a key proof point for the third engine.

title: SAARTHI Scorecard: Strong on Vision and Execution (contd.)
subtitle: Reliance scores an impressive 79/100, reflecting its visionary strategy and strong execution, reinforcing our BUY recommendation.

blocks.title: Key Risks to Thesis

items.title: Execution Delays in New Energy
items.body: The scale of the New Energy ambition is unprecedented; any delays in technology integration or commissioning could defer value creation.

items.title: O2C Commodity Volatility
items.body: The legacy O2C business remains a significant earnings contributor and is exposed to volatile global crude oil prices and refining margins.

items.title: Regulatory & Competitive Headwinds
items.body: Intensifying competition in telecom/retail or adverse policy changes in the energy sector could impact profitability and growth assumptions.
